title: sphenopholis interrupta subsp. californica
intro: Sphenopholis interrupta subsp. californica, also known as California bluegrass, is a type of grass native to the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds, as well as to reduce fever and promote relaxation. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional herbal remedies to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
